records.ety: [L. salutare, salutatum, from salus, -utis, health, safety. See Salubrious.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Saluted; p. pr. & vb. n. Saluting.]

defs: To address, as with expressions of kind wishes and courtesy; to greet; to hail.
quotes: I salute you with this kingly title.

defs: Hence, to give a sign of good will; to compliment by an act or ceremony, as a kiss, a bow, etc.
quotes: You have the prettiest tip of a finger . . . I must take the freedom to salute it.

defs: To honor, as some day, person, or nation, by a discharge of cannon or small arms, by dipping colors, by cheers, etc.
To promote the welfare and safety of; to benefit; to gratify.
The act of saluting, or expressing kind wishes or respect; salutation; greeting.
A sign, token, or ceremony, expressing good will, compliment, or respect, as a kiss, a bow, etc.

A token of respect or honor for some distinguished or official personage, for a foreign vessel or flag, or for some festival or event, as by presenting arms, by a discharge of cannon, volleys of small arms, dipping the colors or the topsails, etc.
